mission.title: Install and Configure Oauth2 Proxy on Kubernetes
mission.description: OAuth2 Proxy is a flexible, open-source tool that acts as a reverse proxy providing authentication with various OAuth2 and OIDC providers. Installing it allows you to secure your web applications easily with OAuth2 authentication.

steps.title: Add the OAuth2 Proxy Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the official OAuth2 Proxy Helm repository to your Helm client:
```bash
helm repo add oauth2-proxy https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OAuth2 Proxy using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install OAuth2 Proxy with the specified version in a new namespace:
```bash
helm install oauth2-proxy oauth2-proxy/oauth2-proxy --version 7.14.3 --namespace oauth2-proxy --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OAuth2 Proxy pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace oauth2-proxy
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To ensure your OAuth2 Proxy has adequate resources, apply resource limits:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/oauth2-proxy --namespace oauth2-proxy --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the service
steps.description:
To access the OAuth2 Proxy service locally, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/oauth2-proxy --namespace oauth2-proxy 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the OAuth2 Proxy pod running in the 'oauth2-proxy' namespace, and you will be able to access it via port 8080 on your local machine. You can further configure it to suit your authentication needs.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add oauth2-proxy https://oauth2-proxy.github.io/oauth2-proxy
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy OAuth2 Proxy.
